title: Gaultheria triangulanthera
intro: Gaultheria triangulanthera, also known as the Mountain Tea or Triangular-leaved Gaultheria, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. Traditionally, the leaves of Gaultheria triangulanthera have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including triterpenoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Gaultheria triangulanthera is commonly prepared as a tea, and its leaves are also used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall health.
